title: phanera moultonii
intro: Phanera moultonii, also known as the "Roxburgh's tree orchid", is a type of orchid native to the Himalayas and Southeast Asia. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, and has been traditionally used to treat various health issues such as fever, cough, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Phanera moultonii include its ability to reduce inflammation, boost the immune system, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections. The herbal preparations of Phanera moultonii include decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to create teas, tonics, and other medicinal products.
